annotations.label: Six (natural number)

anchor.snippet: is the natural number following 5 and preceding 7

annotations.note: The natural number 6 exists as the literal `6 : ℕ` via Mathlib's standard `Nat` type.

annotations.label: 6 is composite and the smallest perfect number

anchor.snippet: It is a composite number and the smallest perfect number

annotations.note: `Nat.Perfect` is defined in Mathlib but `Perfect 6` and the minimality claim are not stated as theorems.

annotations.label: Hexagon

anchor.snippet: A six-sided polygon is a hexagon

annotations.note: No general polygon/hexagon definition was found in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Hexagon tiles the plane

anchor.snippet: one of the three regular polygons capable of tiling the plane

annotations.note: No formalization of regular polygon tilings of the plane appears in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Hexagon edge/angle count

anchor.snippet: A hexagon also has 6 edges as well as 6 internal and external angles

annotations.note: Mathlib has no hexagon datatype to count edges/angles against.

annotations.label: 6 is the second smallest composite

anchor.snippet: 6 is the second smallest composite number

annotations.note: No predicate or theorem about the ordering of composite numbers placing 6 second was located.

annotations.label: 6 is the smallest perfect number

anchor.snippet: the first number that is the sum of its proper divisors, making it the smallest perfect number

annotations.note: `Nat.Perfect` is available but no proof of `Perfect 6` or its minimality was found.

annotations.label: 6 unique perfect number without digital root 1

anchor.snippet: the only perfect number that doesn't have a digital root of 1

annotations.note: No digital-root machinery applied to perfect numbers is present in Mathlib.

annotations.label: 6 is the first unitary perfect number

anchor.snippet: 6 is the first unitary perfect number

annotations.note: Unitary perfect numbers are not defined in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Only five unitary perfect numbers known

anchor.snippet: Only five such numbers are known to exist

annotations.note: No notion of unitary perfect numbers exists in Mathlib.

annotations.label: 6 largest all-Harshad number

anchor.snippet: 6 is the largest of the four all-Harshad numbers

annotations.note: Harshad numbers are not formalized in Mathlib.

annotations.label: 6 as superior highly composite, triangular, etc.

anchor.snippet: 6 is the 2nd superior highly composite number

annotations.note: No notion of superior highly composite numbers found in Mathlib.

annotations.label: 6 is the first Granville number

anchor.snippet: 6 is also the first Granville number

annotations.note: Granville numbers are not formalized in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Golomb ruler of length 6

anchor.snippet: A Golomb ruler of length 6 is a "perfect ruler"

annotations.note: Golomb rulers are not defined in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Six exponentials theorem

anchor.snippet: The six exponentials theorem guarantees that under certain conditions one of a set of six exponentials is transcendental

annotations.note: Grep for `sixExponentials` and variants in Mathlib returned no results.

annotations.label: Smallest non-abelian group has 6 elements

anchor.snippet: The smallest non- abelian group is the symmetric group

annotations.note: `Equiv.Perm (Fin 3)` exists and is known non-abelian in Mathlib, but the minimality statement among non-abelian groups was not located.

annotations.label: Two-dimensional kissing number is 6

anchor.snippet: 6 the answer to the two-dimensional kissing number problem

annotations.note: Kissing numbers are not formalized in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Cube has 6 faces

anchor.snippet: A cube has 6 faces

annotations.note: No face-count statement about the cube was found in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Tetrahedron has 6 edges

anchor.snippet: A tetrahedron has 6 edges

annotations.note: No tetrahedron edge-count formalization was found in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Six convex regular polytopes in 4D

anchor.snippet: there are a total of six convex regular polytopes

annotations.note: Convex regular polytopes are not classified in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Sporadic groups and pariahs

anchor.snippet: twenty of twenty-six sporadic groups in the happy family

annotations.note: Sporadic simple groups and the happy family are not formalized in Mathlib.

annotations.label: No finite field of size 6

anchor.snippet: 6 is the smallest integer which is not an exponent of a prime number

annotations.note: Mathlib's `FieldTheory.Cardinality` characterizes finite field sizes as prime powers, which implies no field of size 6, though the specialization is not stated.

annotations.label: Hexahedron

anchor.snippet: A hexahedron is a polyhedron with six faces

annotations.note: No hexahedron/polyhedron definition was located in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Senary

anchor.snippet: Senary is the ordinal adjective meaning "sixth"

annotations.note: Base-6/senary numeration is not specifically formalized in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Sexy prime pairs

anchor.snippet: Prime pairs differing by six are sexy

annotations.note: Sexy prime pairs are not defined in Mathlib.

annotations.label: SI prefix exa-/atto-

anchor.snippet: The SI prefix for 1000 6 is exa- (E), and for its reciprocal atto- (a)

annotations.note: SI prefixes are outside Mathlib's mathematical scope.
